title: Football - The Kathmandu Post
summary: Football FIFA, AFC press NSC to lift ANFA suspension, resume election process By Himesh World football bodies warn of possible suspension if interference persists, reject call for tier-based polls. New trial over soccer legend Maradona’s death begins in Argentina By Reuters A court in ​San Isidro, near Buenos Aires, will hear testimony from just under 100 witnesses ​as it tries Maradona’s medical
content:
Football FIFA, AFC press NSC to lift ANFA suspension, resume election process By Himesh World football bodies warn of possible suspension if interference persists, reject call for tier-based polls. New trial over soccer legend Maradona’s death begins in Argentina By Reuters A court in ​San Isidro, near Buenos Aires, will hear testimony from just under 100 witnesses ​as it tries Maradona’s medical team over alleged negligence in the death ⁠of the 1986 World Cup champion. Italy miss out on World Cup for third straight time after shootout loss to Bosnia By Reuters Bosnia fight back with 10-man Italy forced to penalties as Barbarez’s side seal historic World Cup berth, extending Azzurri’s long qualifying drought. Nepal’s joint-highest goalscorer Bista launches football academy By Nayak Paudel Anjan Bista Training Centre is organising ‘Next Gen Boot Camp’, a 10-day football training session for teenagers aged eight to 16 from April 4 to 13. Arena to test his Nepali team against Laos today By Nayak Paudel It will be Arena’s first international game as the head coach of Nepal. A former head coach of Laos, Arena wants to start his campaign with a win and make Nepali fans back home happy. What procedures do FIFA and AFC follow before suspending a member association? By Post Report With Nepali football at a standstill following NSC’s suspension of ANFA, the threat of a FIFA suspension looms large. But how do international bodies actually decide when to act? UEFA-pro Arena in a no-pro zone of Nepali football By Nayak Paudel New Nepal men’s head coach lost the chance to test his team at home against Hong Kong on Thursday. Now, Nepal’s AFC Asian Cup Qualifiers fixture against Laos, scheduled for March 31, is also at risk with ‘suspended ANFA’ raising hands. National Sports Council suspends ANFA for three months By Nayak Paudel The two bodies had been at loggerheads for months over ANFA’s decision to conduct the elections of its executive body early. The tussle now risks ANFA’s suspension from FIFA. Police beat APF by 185 runs while defending record total By Post Report Bagmati defeated Gandaki by 170 runs in the other match of the Men’s PM Cup on Tuesday. ANFA closed for March 27 election By Post Report The National Sports Council asks the footballing body to submit a clarification within 24 hours after going against the order to suspend early election processes. Nepali football hits another low as ANFA, NSC trade blame By Post Report Clubs left in limbo as ANFA-NSC standoff over visas, elections and stadium access disrupts domestic and international fixtures. World Cup ticket frenzy unfolds amid global unrest By Reuters FIFA has said nearly two million tickets were sold in the first two sales phases, with demand so intense that World Cup tickets were oversubscribed over 30 times. Title race wide open as National League reaches midpoint By Nayak Paudel Two teams stay unbeaten and eight have a shot at the title, while off-field legal battles add intrigue to ANFA’s expanded competition. Sankata cruise past Chitlang with hattrick from Onyema By Post Report Laligurans Association Club and Friends’ Club Kopundole settled for a 1-1 draw in the other match of the day. Three Star Club breathe a sigh of relief after Patan High Court order By Nayak Paudel The court on Wednesday overturned the ANFA Appeal Committee’s decision that led to Three Star’s relegation from the A Division. APF, Laligurans firm on top two positions By Post Report APF beat Manang 2-0 to remain top, Laligurans thrash Kankrebihar 4-1 as Haule nets fastest hat-trick of the tournament. How much will Ninja A-League insurance cover for Samba’s treatment? By Nayak Paudel Responding to an email query from the Post, Wellington Phoenix said the league insurance would cover surgical and rehabilitation costs up to 20,000 Australian dollars. Why did Samba choose Aspetar above all others? By Nayak Paudel Experts weigh in on the athlete’s decision to seek cure at the Qatar-based hospital while facilities in Nepal had offered free treatment. Church Boys defeat Army. Friends and Chitlang play goalless draw By Post Report There are three matches scheduled for Wednesday, all to be played at Dasharath Stadium. Wellington Phoenix backs Samba’s $80,000 surgery plea as insurance falls short By Nayak Paudel Responding to the Post, Wellington Phoenix confirms the insurance payout will not cover the estimated $80,000 cost of surgery and rehab at Aspetar, but the club is backing her fundraising appeal and wants to see her back in yellow and black.

By Himesh

World football bodies warn of possible suspension if interference persists, reject call for tier-based polls.

By Reuters

A court in ​San Isidro, near Buenos Aires, will hear testimony from just under 100 witnesses ​as it tries Maradona’s medical team over alleged negligence in the death ⁠of the 1986 World Cup champion.

By Reuters

Bosnia fight back with 10-man Italy forced to penalties as Barbarez’s side seal historic World Cup berth, extending Azzurri’s long qualifying drought.

By Nayak Paudel

Anjan Bista Training Centre is organising ‘Next Gen Boot Camp’, a 10-day football training session for teenagers aged eight to 16 from April 4 to 13.

By Nayak Paudel

It will be Arena’s first international game as the head coach of Nepal. A former head coach of Laos, Arena wants to start his campaign with a win and make Nepali fans back home happy.

By Post Report

With Nepali football at a standstill following NSC’s suspension of ANFA, the threat of a FIFA suspension looms large. But how do international bodies actually decide when to act?

By Nayak Paudel

New Nepal men’s head coach lost the chance to test his team at home against Hong Kong on Thursday. Now, Nepal’s AFC Asian Cup Qualifiers fixture against Laos, scheduled for March 31, is also at risk with ‘suspended ANFA’ raising hands.

By Nayak Paudel

The two bodies had been at loggerheads for months over ANFA’s decision to conduct the elections of its executive body early. The tussle now risks ANFA’s suspension from FIFA.

By Post Report

Bagmati defeated Gandaki by 170 runs in the other match of the Men’s PM Cup on Tuesday.

By Post Report

The National Sports Council asks the footballing body to submit a clarification within 24 hours after going against the order to suspend early election processes.

By Post Report

Clubs left in limbo as ANFA-NSC standoff over visas, elections and stadium access disrupts domestic and international fixtures.

By Reuters

FIFA has said nearly two million tickets were sold in the first two sales phases, with demand so intense that World Cup tickets were oversubscribed over 30 times.

By Nayak Paudel

Two teams stay unbeaten and eight have a shot at the title, while off-field legal battles add intrigue to ANFA’s expanded competition.

By Post Report

Laligurans Association Club and Friends’ Club Kopundole settled for a 1-1 draw in the other match of the day.

By Nayak Paudel

The court on Wednesday overturned the ANFA Appeal Committee’s decision that led to Three Star’s relegation from the A Division.

By Post Report

APF beat Manang 2-0 to remain top, Laligurans thrash Kankrebihar 4-1 as Haule nets fastest hat-trick of the tournament.

By Nayak Paudel

Responding to an email query from the Post, Wellington Phoenix said the league insurance would cover surgical and rehabilitation costs up to 20,000 Australian dollars.

By Nayak Paudel

Experts weigh in on the athlete’s decision to seek cure at the Qatar-based hospital while facilities in Nepal had offered free treatment.

By Post Report

There are three matches scheduled for Wednesday, all to be played at Dasharath Stadium.

By Nayak Paudel

Responding to the Post, Wellington Phoenix confirms the insurance payout will not cover the estimated $80,000 cost of surgery and rehab at Aspetar, but the club is backing her fundraising appeal and wants to see her back in yellow and black.
